description: Fix 4 bugs and add 2 improvements to the STP Application: (1) auto-snapshot before applyBrainPlan, (2) persist and pre-fill project description, (3) filter Route Progress view by detected countries, (4) fix What-If timeline critical-path calculation, (5) show diff summary after re-analysis.
steps: BUG 3 - Auto-snapshot before reanalysis: In qi-webapp/js/ui.js function applyBrainPlan (line ~3738), at the very top before clearing any data, add: if (S.get().cases && S.get().cases.length > 0) { S.takeSnapshot('Auto-snapshot before reanalysis'); }. This gives users a one-click restore point from History & Backups.

BUG 2 - Project description persistence: (a) In qi-webapp/js/store.js function normalize(), add: if (typeof s.brainDescription !== 'string') s.brainDescription = ''; (b) Add two new API functions: setBrainDescription(text) that sets state.brainDescription = text and calls save(), and getBrainDescription() that returns state.brainDescription. Export both in the API object. (c) In qi-webapp/js/ui.js AFTER.brain, after the existing plan restoration logic, add: if (S.getBrainDescription && S.getBrainDescription()) ta.value = S.getBrainDescription(); (d) In runAnalyze(), after 'const text = ta.value.trim()', add: if (S.setBrainDescription) S.setBrainDescription(text); (e) In the file upload handlers (DOCX, PDF, and plain text), after setting ta.value = txt, add: if (S.setBrainDescription) S.setBrainDescription(txt);

BUG 1 - Route Progress filtering: In qi-webapp/js/ui.js RENDER.routeprogress (line ~2064), after getting cables from G.CABLES, add filtering: const scopeIds = activeStationIds(); const filteredCables = scopeIds ? cables.filter(c => scopeIds.indexOf(c.from) !== -1 || scopeIds.indexOf(c.to) !== -1) : cables; Then use filteredCables for the cards rendering (the per-segment cards). Keep the unfiltered cables array available for the rollup chart which should show the full programme. Also filter in AFTER.routeprogress similarly.

BUG 4 - What-If timeline critical-path fix: In qi-webapp/js/store.js function whatIf(), replace the naive timeline calculation (var scenarioMonths = Math.round(baseMonths * (activeCables.length / (cables.length || 1))) + permitDelay;) with critical-path logic: (a) Identify trunk cables (IDs starting with 'STP-T') vs branch cables (IDs starting with 'STP-B'). (b) Count total trunk segments and remaining active trunk segments. (c) Timeline formula: scenarioMonths = Math.round(baseMonths * (activeTrunkCount / totalTrunkCount)) + permitDelay. (d) Branches run in parallel so removing them does NOT reduce the timeline. (e) When no trunk segments remain, timeline = permitDelay (all critical work is removed). (f) totalTrunkCount is the total number of trunk cables in the full cable set (5 for STP-T1 through STP-T5).

IMPROVEMENT 1 - Diff summary after re-analysis: In qi-webapp/js/ui.js function applyBrainPlan(), before clearing data, capture prevCounts = { cases: (st0.cases || []).length, milestones: (st0.registers.milestones || []).length, procurement: (st0.registers.procurement || []).length, stakeholders: (st0.stakeholders || []).length }. After the plan is applied, build a diffSummary string showing what changed: 'Plan regenerated: X tasks (was Y), Z milestones (was W)...' and 'Auto-snapshot saved - you can roll back from History & Backups if needed.' Store this as a return value or attach to the plan object so the toast or brain preview can show it. The existing toast in runAnalyze can be enhanced to mention the diff.

Add smoke test assertions: In qi-webapp/smoke.js, add tests for each fix: (a) Bug 3: after analyzing, verify snapshots exist with label containing 'reanalysis'. (b) Bug 2: verify setBrainDescription/getBrainDescription exist and persist text. (c) Bug 1: verify that RENDER.routeprogress filters cables when brainPlan.countryIntel is set. (d) Bug 4: verify whatIf() timeline math uses trunk-based critical path - removing a branch should not reduce timeline, removing a trunk should. (e) Improvement 1: verify applyBrainPlan returns or exposes a diff summary.
Run npm test to verify all existing 695+ assertions still pass plus the new ones. Run node verify-standalone.js to confirm boot checks pass.
acceptance_criteria: Running npm test passes ALL existing assertions plus new assertions for each fix
S.takeSnapshot is called before clearing data in applyBrainPlan when cases.length > 0
setBrainDescription(text) persists and getBrainDescription() retrieves the project description text
AFTER.brain pre-fills the textarea with the persisted brainDescription
RENDER.routeprogress only shows cable segments connected to detected countries (uses activeStationIds())
whatIf() timeline uses trunk-based critical path: removing STP-B1 does NOT change timeline, removing STP-T3 DOES reduce timeline
applyBrainPlan captures previous counts and produces a diff summary showing what changed
node verify-standalone.js passes all 23 boot checks
findings: All 4 bug fixes and improvement implemented successfully. All existing smoke tests pass plus 28 new assertions. The whatIf timeline now uses trunk-based critical-path logic (STP-T* cables are sequential, STP-B* are parallel). Route progress cards are filtered by activeStationIds() when a brainPlan with countryIntel is present. Auto-snapshot is taken before reanalysis when cases exist. brainDescription is persisted and pre-filled. applyBrainPlan returns {count, diffSummary} instead of just a number.
